laws.title: Illinois Paid Leave for All Workers
laws.summary: Illinois requires all employers to provide employees up to 40 hours of paid leave per 12-month period, which employees may use for any reason — no explanation required. Employees earn 1 hour of paid leave for every 40 hours worked. There are no employer size exemptions; all employers in Illinois must comply.
compliance_actions: Adopt a written paid leave policy stating the accrual rate (1 hour per 40 hours worked, up to 40 hours/year) and permitted uses
Track accrual and usage separately from other leave types and provide employees access to their current balance
Do not require employees to give a reason for taking paid leave — usage for any purpose is protected
Retain payroll and leave records for a minimum of 3 years

laws.title: Chicago Agency Proposes Changes to Fair Workweek Rules
laws.summary:
Chicago Agency Proposes Changes to Fair Workweek Rules 
 The Chicago Office of Labor Standards (OLS) has proposed changes to the rules implementing the city’s Fair Workweek Ordinance. 1  The Ordinance requires covered employers to provide advance notice of work schedules to their covered employees, and to pay additional wages if posted schedules are changed within a certain time period.
